feed.title: International Journal of the Sociology of Language
feed.subtitle: Latest articles from International Journal of the Sociology of Language (De Gruyter)

entry.title: “Ahora está más complicado”. Narrativas del control de la migración centroamericana por México hacia Estados Unidos
entry.summary: Authors: Francisco Daniel Morales Hernández DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2024-0139 Pages: Page range: 1-24

entry.title: New speakers of the Basque informal address formhika, the quintessential marker of authenticity
entry.summary: Authors: Beñat Muguruza, Garbiñe Bereziartua DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0005 Pages: Page range: 25-59

entry.title: Competing patterns of prestige in Latin American Spanish: implicit attitudes towards informal address in two Uruguayan cities
entry.summary: Authors: María Irene Moyna, Verónica Loureiro-Rodríguez, Elif Fidan Acar DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0058 Pages: Page range: 61-99

entry.title: Thai-Muslim young adults’ narrative identities as multilingual learners: intersections of individualization, language ideologies, and ethnic accent bullying
entry.summary: Authors: Melada Sudajit-apa, Alisa Hasamoh DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0063 Pages: Page range: 101-131

entry.title: The “total linguistic fact” of Ulti, the secret language of the hijra-kotis in Bengal
entry.summary: Authors: Ayesha Kidwai, Enakshi Nandi DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0082 Pages: Page range: 133-152

entry.title: Family dynamics, community ties, and strategies in the transmission of Japanese as a heritage language in Barcelona
entry.summary: Authors: Isabel Sáenz-Hernández DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2024-0093 Pages: Page range: 153-173
